task_updates.executor_notes: Added `ReconcileDecision` and `PaymentService.reconcile_with_chain(...)` in the post-cleanup PaymentService. The method now covers `reconciled_confirmed`, `reconciled_failed`, `allow_requeue`, `keep_in_hold`, and `not_applicable`, uses `now - submitted_at` for the 150s blockhash heuristic, and treats provider exceptions or `rpc_unreachable` as `keep_in_hold` without mutating state. Unit coverage in `tests/test_payment_reconcile.py` exercises all required branches.

Added `db_handler.get_legacy_provider_payment_requests(guild_ids)`, `PaymentService.migrate_legacy_provider_rows(guild_ids)`, and the worker startup hook in `PaymentWorkerCog.cog_load`. The migration rewrites `grants -> solana_grants`, `admin_chat -> solana_payouts`, and marks unknown producers `manual_hold` with the requested reason while logging one WARNING per legacy row. Tests cover the DB selector, the three migration branches, and the one-time worker startup call.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Verified `reconcile_with_chain` covers `reconciled_confirmed`, `reconciled_failed`, `allow_requeue`, `keep_in_hold`, `not_applicable`, and `rpc_unreachable -> keep_in_hold` with dedicated unit coverage. The 150s blockhash heuristic is implemented as `datetime.now(timezone.utc) - submitted_at`.

Verified `migrate_legacy_provider_rows` handles all three branches (`grants -> solana_grants`, `admin_chat -> solana_payouts`, `unknown -> manual_hold`), and `PaymentWorkerCog.cog_load` now calls the migration once at startup. Fake-DB tests cover both the migration behavior and the startup hook.
